rules.description: Kubernetes workloads will no longer be supported with Windows Server 2019 when Kubernetes version 1.32 reaches End of Life (EOL).
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation

rules.description: Use custom autoscale for Virtual Machine Scale Sets based on metrics and schedules to improve performance and cost effectiveness, adjusting instances as demand changes.
rules.fix: Use custom autoscale for Virtual Machine Scale Sets based on metrics and schedules to improve performance and cost effectiveness

rules.description: Microsoft advises disabling strictly even VM instance distribution across Availability Zones in VMSS to improve scalability and flexibility, noting that uneven distribution may better serve application load demands despite the potential trade-off in resilience.
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation

rules.description: Predictive autoscale utilizes machine learning to efficiently manage and scale Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets by forecasting CPU load through historical usage analysis, ensuring timely scale-out to meet demand.
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation

rules.description: Windows Server 2022 retires when Kubernetes 1.34 reaches the end of platform support. On Kubernetes 1.35 and later, creation of new Windows Server 2022 node pools is blocked.
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation

rules.description: Enabling automatic instance repairs helps achieve high availability by maintaining a set of healthy instances. If an unhealthy instance is found by the Application Health extension or load balancer health probe, automatic instance repairs attempt to recover the instance by triggering repair actions.
rules.query: where isnotnull(properties.virtualMachineProfile) | where isnull(properties.automaticRepairsPolicy) or properties.automaticRepairsPolicy.enabled == false
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation

rules.description: Windows Server 2019 retires when Kubernetes 1.32 reaches the end of platform support. On Kubernetes 1.33 and later, creation of new Windows Server 2019 node pools is blocked.
rules.fix: Review and remediate based on recommendation
